subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Plankton Density & Particle Motion
stimulus: Researchers on a boat are investigating plankton cells in a sea. At a depth of $h$ meters, the density of plankton cells, in millions of cells per cubic meter, is modeled by $p(h) = 0.2h^2 e^{-0.0025h^2}$ for $0 \leq h \leq 30$ and is modeled by $f(h)$ for $h \geq 30$. The continuous function $f$ is not explicitly given.

parts.prompt: Find $p'(25)$. Using correct units, interpret the meaning of $p'(25)$ in the context of the problem.

scoring_points.description: Correct derivative value: differentiating $p(h) = 0.2h^2 e^{-0.0025h^2}$ gives $p'(h) = 0.4h\,e^{-0.0025h^2} + 0.2h^2(-0.005h)e^{-0.0025h^2} = e^{-0.0025h^2}(0.4h - 0.001h^3)$. Evaluated at $h = 25$: $p'(25) \approx -0.299$ million cells per cubic meter per meter.

required_elements: $p'(25) \approx -0.299$ million cells per cubic meter per meter

scoring_points.description: Correct interpretation with units: At a depth of $25$ meters, the density of plankton cells is decreasing at a rate of approximately $0.299$ million cells per cubic meter per meter of depth.

required_elements: The density of plankton cells is decreasing at approximately $0.299$ million cells per cubic meter per meter of depth at $h = 25$ meters

parts.prompt: Consider a vertical column of water in this sea with horizontal cross sections of constant area $3$ square meters. To the nearest million, how many plankton cells are in this column of water between $h = 0$ and $h = 30$ meters?

scoring_points.description: Sets up the correct integral: total plankton (millions) $= 3\int_0^{30} p(h)\,dh = 3\int_0^{30} 0.2h^2 e^{-0.0025h^2}\,dh$
Correct answer: $3\int_0^{30} p(h)\,dh \approx 3(561.647\ldots) \approx 1685$ million plankton cells (to the nearest million).

parts.prompt: There is a function $u$ such that $0 \leq f(h) \leq u(h)$ for all $h \geq 30$ and $\int_{30}^{\infty} u(h)\,dh = 105$. The column of water in part (b) is $K$ meters deep, where $K > 30$. Write an expression involving one or more integrals that gives the number of plankton cells, in millions, in the entire column. Explain why the number of plankton cells in the column is less than or equal to $2000$ million.

scoring_points.description: Correct expression for total plankton in a column of depth $K > 30$: $3\int_0^{30} p(h)\,dh + 3\int_{30}^{K} f(h)\,dh$ (millions of cells)
Uses the bound $0 \leq f(h) \leq u(h)$ to write: total plankton $\leq 3\int_0^{30} p(h)\,dh + 3\int_{30}^{\infty} u(h)\,dh$

Completes the bound: $3\int_0^{30} p(h)\,dh + 3\int_{30}^{\infty} u(h)\,dh \approx 3(561.647) + 3(105) \approx 1684.9 + 315 < 2000$. Since $3\int_0^{30} p(h)\,dh \approx 1685$ and $3 \cdot 105 = 315$, the total is at most approximately $1685 + 315 = 2000$, so the number of plankton cells in the column is less than or equal to $2000$ million.

parts.prompt: The boat is moving on the surface of the sea. At time $t \geq 0$, the position of the boat is $(x(t), y(t))$, where $x'(t) = 662\sin(5t)$ and $y'(t) = 880\cos(6t)$. Time $t$ is measured in hours, and $x(t)$ and $y(t)$ are measured in meters. Find the total distance traveled by the boat over the time interval $0 \leq t \leq 1$.

scoring_points.description: Sets up the correct arc length integral for total distance: $\int_0^1 \sqrt{[x'(t)]^2 + [y'(t)]^2}\,dt = \int_0^1 \sqrt{(662\sin 5t)^2 + (880\cos 6t)^2}\,dt$
